risk.desc: Potential thermal issues due to insufficient heat sink size.
risk.mit: Increase heat sink dimensions or add active cooling.

risk.desc: Risk of electrical interference if power and signal lines are not properly separated.
risk.mit: Ensure power and signal lines are routed separately with adequate spacing.

risk.desc: Tool-less lid may not provide sufficient protection for internal components.
risk.mit: Evaluate lid design for improved protection or consider adding a protective layer.

risk.desc: Insufficient clearance around connectors could lead to mechanical stress.
risk.mit: Increase clearance around connectors to reduce mechanical stress.

risk.desc: Snap-fit mounting may not be robust enough under repeated use.
risk.mit: Consider alternative mounting methods or reinforce snap-fit design.

risk.desc: Potential overhang issues during FDM printing may affect hinge_pin stability.
risk.mit: Implement support structures under critical overhangs.

risk.mit: Include alignment guides during assembly to ensure proper positioning.

generated_parts.label: Wiring Loom + Service Loop Set
generated_parts.spec: Knuckle-crossing wiring loom with service loops, strain relief at every flex boundary, flex-rated bend radius, power vs signal channel separation

requirements: Knuckle-crossing wiring with service loops and flex-rated bend radius
Service loops, strain relief, bend radius note
Per-finger linkage cartridges removable by single pin or visible hinge access
Actuator modules plug in and remain visible near knuckle row
Rigid mock assembly preserves soft-contact cues, wrist quick release, and ergonomic clearance
Finger and wrist wiring uses service loops, strain relief at every flex boundary, and a stated bend radius sized for repeated flexion cycles
Motor power lines and sensor/encoder signal lines run in separate channels or are spaced/crossed at right angles
Each electronics module states its grounding scheme (star point, chassis bond, isolated)
PCBs mount on standoffs with keep-out clearance to walls and moving parts
Every inter-part interface names a specific connection type with its DOF and a numeric clearance
Parts can be installed in a stated order where every fastener, connector, and mating feature stays reachable
One primary datum part anchors the assembly; each other part locates off a named parent datum using a 3-2-1 scheme
Every part's primary load traces through named interfaces to the base/ground
The assembly uses the fewest interfaces that still allow service and manufacture
Critical joints need visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent
